questions.text: Which organisation controls the majority of the world's oil production and is headquartered in Vienna?
questions.explanation: OPEC (Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries), founded in 1960, is headquartered in Vienna, Austria. It has 13 member countries (including Saudi Arabia, UAE, Iraq, Iran, Kuwait, Nigeria) and controls approximately 40% of global oil production.

questions.text: Which city in India is called the 'Silicon Valley of India'?
questions.explanation: Bengaluru (Bangalore) in Karnataka is called the 'Silicon Valley of India' due to its concentration of IT and software companies, including headquarters of Infosys, Wipro, TCS, and major offices of global tech firms.

questions.text: The Strait of Malacca is the world's most important shipping strait because:
options: It connects the Indian Ocean to the Pacific/South China Sea, carrying ~25% of global trade
It is controlled by the US Navy
It is the shortest route from Europe to Africa
questions.explanation: The Strait of Malacca (between Malaysia and Indonesia's Sumatra) is the world's busiest shipping strait, connecting the Indian Ocean to the South China Sea/Pacific. About 25% of world trade and a significant portion of the world's oil supply passes through it.

questions.text: Which country is the world's largest producer of coal?
questions.explanation: China is by far the world's largest coal producer, accounting for about 50% of global coal production. India is 2nd, followed by Indonesia and Australia. China also consumes the most coal domestically.

questions.text: The Suez Canal differs from the Panama Canal in that the Suez Canal:
options: Has no locks (is at sea level)
questions.explanation: The Suez Canal (Egypt, 193 km) is at sea level and requires NO locks — ships sail straight through. The Panama Canal (80 km) uses three sets of locks to raise ships up to Gatun Lake (~26 m above sea level) and then lower them. Suez connects two seas; Panama connects two oceans.

questions.text: Which country is the world's top iron ore producer?
questions.explanation: Australia is the world's largest iron ore producer (and exporter), led by the Pilbara region in Western Australia. Brazil is 2nd (Carajás mine — world's largest iron ore mine, operated by Vale). India and China also produce significant quantities.

questions.text: Ahmedabad is called the 'Manchester of India' because of its:
questions.explanation: Ahmedabad (Gujarat) earned the title 'Manchester of India' due to its large cotton textile industry, historically concentrated along the Sabarmati River. Manchester (UK) was the historic centre of Britain's cotton textile industry during the Industrial Revolution.

questions.text: Which country is the largest LNG (Liquefied Natural Gas) exporter in the world?
questions.explanation: Qatar is the world's largest LNG exporter, with its massive North Dome/South Pars gas field (shared with Iran). Russia has the largest natural gas reserves, but Qatar leads in LNG exports. Australia has been competing for the top LNG export slot in recent years.

questions.text: Which mineral is India's #1 globally, produced mainly in Jharkhand and Andhra Pradesh?
questions.explanation: India is the world's largest producer of mica — the shiny, heat-resistant mineral used in electrical insulation and cosmetics. Jharkhand and Andhra Pradesh are the main producing states. India accounts for a dominant share of global mica supply.

questions.text: The 'Ruhr Valley' in Germany is famous for which industry?
questions.explanation: The Ruhr Valley (North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany) was Europe's industrial heartland, powered by local coal and steel production. Today it has diversified, but historically Ruhr coal + Rhine transport + iron ore = major steel production.

questions.text: South Korea is the world's leading nation in which heavy industry?
questions.explanation: South Korea is the world's #1 shipbuilder by tonnage, with companies like Hyundai Heavy Industries, Samsung Heavy Industries, and DSME. South Korea overtook Japan as the global shipbuilding leader and is also a major automobile producer (Hyundai, Kia).

questions.text: Which of the following is correctly matched (mineral → top world producer)?
questions.explanation: Kazakhstan is the world's #1 uranium producer, accounting for ~40% of global output. Chile has the largest lithium reserves (Lithium Triangle) but Australia now produces the most lithium. South Africa is top in platinum; DRC/Botswana lead in diamonds by different metrics. India is not a top copper producer.

questions.text: The city of Pittsburgh (USA) was historically known as 'Steel City' because of its proximity to:
options: Appalachian coal fields and Great Lakes iron ore
questions.explanation: Pittsburgh's iron and steel industry grew from its ideal location near Appalachian coal fields and access to iron ore from the Great Lakes region. Andrew Carnegie built his steel empire here. The industry has since declined, but Pittsburgh's geographic logic exemplifies resource-based industrial location.

questions.text: Which is the world's top coffee producing country?
questions.explanation: Brazil is the world's largest coffee producer by a wide margin (~35–40% of global production). Vietnam is 2nd largest. Colombia is famous for quality but ranks 3rd in production. Ethiopia is the birthplace of coffee but a smaller producer.

questions.text: The 'Bhilai Steel Plant' in Chhattisgarh was established with assistance from which country?
questions.explanation: Bhilai Steel Plant (1959, Chhattisgarh) was established with Soviet (USSR) assistance. Durgapur Steel Plant (1959, West Bengal) had British assistance, and Rourkela Steel Plant (1959, Odisha) had West German assistance — each during India's Second Five-Year Plan.

questions.text: Which Indian city is the hub of India's diamond cutting and polishing industry?
questions.explanation: Surat (Gujarat) is the world's largest diamond cutting and polishing centre — processing ~90% of the world's rough diamonds. Jaipur is known for coloured gemstones (emeralds, rubies). Mumbai is the financial and commodities trading hub.
